findings.title: shelvesDone deadlock risk when shelves entry is skipSync/uploadOnly
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the pre-check on line ~978 to also resolve `shelvesResolve()` when the `shelves` entry exists but is skipSync or uploadOnly. Alternatively, move `shelvesResolve()` into the skipSync/uploadOnly early-return path inside `processEntry` so the resolver fires regardless of whether the task actually ran.
findings.why: `processEntry` returns early on `skipSync` or `uploadOnly` BEFORE reaching the `finally` block where `shelvesResolve()` is called. The pre-check at line 978 only handles the case where the `shelves` entry is absent from the registry — it does NOT fire when the entry is present but flagged skipSync/uploadOnly. The `bookShelves` task then awaits `shelvesDone` forever, deadlocking the entire download since Promise.all never resolves.
evidence: Line ~978: `if (!tableRegistry.some(e => e.dexieTable === 'shelves' && !e.skipSync && !e.uploadOnly)) shelvesResolve()` — resolves only when NO matching entry is found
processEntry early-returns at `if (entry.skipSync) return` and `if (entry.uploadOnly) return` before the try/finally
shelvesResolve is only invoked inside the finally block under `if (entry.dexieTable === 'shelves')`

findings.title: TooltipProvider instantiated per-render inside conditional IIFE
findings.suggested_fix: Hoist <TooltipProvider> to a higher stable level (Layout root or Library top-level) rather than wrapping a single button. shadcn/ui guidance recommends one provider per app. The current placement recreates the provider context each render and is inconsistent with how tooltips are used elsewhere in the app.
findings.why: A fresh TooltipProvider wrapping only one trigger on each render inflates React tree churn and breaks the shared delay/behavior contract if multiple tooltips later land on the same page. Also, other tooltips already rendered elsewhere in the tree may lose coordination.

findings.title: 429 toast dedup set name is misleading — no toast is emitted
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) wire an actual `toast.warning()` call as the plan (R3 + Unit 5) specifies (`429 toast deduplication: toast once per table per session`), or (b) rename `throttleToasted` to `throttleLoggedSet` to match the console.warn-only behavior. Current code has the dedup set but emits only console.warn, not a user-visible toast — diverges from plan.
findings.why: Plan Unit 5 explicitly states: `after exhausted retries … toast fires once (not 3 times).` The implementation substitutes console.warn for the toast. Users will see no indication that their library failed to sync a table after retries.

findings.title: Unit 2 tests not added — test files referenced in plan are missing
findings.suggested_fix: Add the tests specified in the plan: src/db/__tests__/schema.test.ts (v60 migration), src/stores/__tests__/useAudiobookshelfStore-sync.test.ts (Dexie persistence + hydration), src/app/pages/__tests__/Library.auth-failed.test.tsx (auth-failed guard), src/lib/sync/__tests__/syncEngine.download.test.ts (throttle + 429 backoff), src/app/components/library/__tests__/BookCoverImage.test.tsx, tooltip + empty queue tests. None of these appear in the diff.
findings.why: Plan lists test files for each of Units 1-9 under Files > Test. `git diff --name-only` shows no __tests__ files in the diff. Regression guard is absent; plan's verification column is unmet for most units.

findings.title: handleManualSync useCallback dep missing absServers
findings.suggested_fix: The callback reads server status via `useAudiobookshelfStore.getState().servers`, so the hook deps are technically fine — but if the hook-local `absServers` ever diverges from getState() during a render, the behavior is surprising. Consider reading from `absServers` (the subscribed slice) for consistency, or add a code comment explaining why getState() is used here.
findings.why: `getState()` bypasses the subscription so the handler always sees the latest servers, but downstream readers (tooltip logic) use the subscribed `absServers`. Minor consistency concern; not a correctness bug because the handler is fired after state settles.

findings.title: review_records migration column 'note_id TEXT' lacks FK or index for orphan cleanup
findings.suggested_fix: Consider FK to notes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE if review_records are always scoped to an existing note; or accept orphans and document. Current schema allows dangling review_records when a note is deleted.
findings.why: note_id has no referential integrity. The ReviewRecord TypeScript type may treat it as a soft reference, but DB-level absence of FK risks orphaned rows contributing to unbounded growth.

findings.title: AudiobookCoverGlyph re-export is unused and adds surface area
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the `export { Headphones as AudiobookCoverGlyph }` re-export. BookCard already imports Headphones directly from lucide-react and passes it as `fallbackIcon`. The re-export is dead API surface.
findings.why: Diff shows BookCard still imports Headphones from lucide-react (line 17), so the re-export added for caller convenience is unused. Dead exports bloat the module API.

findings.title: absViewMode local state path reference outdated after FormatTabs change
findings.suggested_fix: The inline TODO says `wire format filter into series/collections grouping`. Track this as a follow-up issue so it doesn't get lost in comments.
findings.why: Inline TODOs tend to stay undone. Plan Unit 8 explicitly allowed a no-op for series/collections format filtering but should be tracked separately.
